name: dispersal_no_separate
title: Dispersal - clusters do not separate
question: At gamma_ld=21, do light/dark clusters stay attached (extreme partial sorting) rather than fully separate?
hypothesis: Just below the separation threshold, clusters remain joined despite strong heterotypic tension.
objective: Simulate gamma_ld=21 from a random IC and show clusters do not separate, contrasting with the gamma_ld=27 case.
description: Reproduction of the Fig 27+ example from Glazier & Graner (Phys. Rev. E 47, 2128, 1993). Exact parameters: J_ll=14, J_dd=2, J_ld=29, J_lM=16, J_dM=16 (gamma_ld=+21, gamma_lM=+9, gamma_dM=+15); T=5.0, lambda=1.0, target A_l=40.0, A_d=40.0. Initial condition: equilibrated_random. Figures reproduced: Fig 27.
claim: Clusters remain attached, an extreme partial-sorting case just below the separation threshold (Fig 27).
expected_behavior: Light and dark clusters remain connected at 2000 MCS (contrast Fig 26)

embed_visualizations.name: Reproduced figures
embed_visualizations.title: Dispersal - clusters do not separate — reproduced figures

findings.statement: Clusters remain attached, an extreme partial-sorting case just below the separation threshold (Fig 27).

conclusion: Reproduced the paper's Dispersal - clusters do not separate example with the exact energies and initial condition; the pbg-cpm CPM engine yields the same qualitative behaviour and topology. Figures: Fig 27.
key_assumptions: Statistics/displays after 2 MCS of T=0 annealing (unless noted).

derived.insight: Clusters remain attached, an extreme partial-sorting case just below the separation threshold (Fig 27).
